delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: Students will work towards developing the ability to exchange opinions, express agreement and disagreement, using simple English in short speech settings.
aims: The goal of this class is to help you get better at talking about issues. In the first semester, you had a chance to share simple ideas and opinions about everyday things. Now, this semester, you will have a chance to select the issues that you think are important, explore the related vocabulary and grammar, and then share your thoughts about those issues. The class will end with a simple team debate about one of these important issues.
goals: Students will gain experience exchanging opinions, expressing agreement and disagreement, and using simple English to make a short speech. [towards CEFR A2.2]
Students will begin to explore voicing their opinions about important topics.
Students will gain experience using English to work in small group settings.
In a simple English debate, students will gain experience making arguments, counterarguments, and rebuttals.

plan.text: Class Introduction, Takyu Eikaiwa, Topic Development
Issues generation Generate questions for issue 1 discussions
Issue 1 discussions, generate questions for issue 2 discussions
Issue 2 discussion, generate questions for issue 2 discussions
Arguments: Introduce the topic and your stance (agree/disagree). Talk briefly about your main points
Counterarguments: Listen to others and explain why you disagree.
Final Full Debate: Opening Arguments, Counterarguments, Rebuttals and Closing Arguments.

textbooks: All material will be prepared by the teacher and distributed online via Teams.
prereq: Students should be able to get across basic ideas and opinions, using simple phrases and sentences (CEFR A2.1)
prep: Students will be required to do pre-reading and pre-writing homework to prepare for effective in-class participation. Failing to do this homework will resutl in a lowered score and will reduce your chance to express your real opionn about important topics.
teachers: ◎ デビット レスリー
